commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bf8e75fc10a13a9bc109a104c5dfd77563360cb2)
commands.review_summary:
The branch passes the detected supervisor into managed update handoff and, for systemd, starts the helper through `systemd-run --user` with mocked tests for systemd launch, failure, and non-systemd fallback.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main combines a plain detached helper spawn with `KillMode=control-group`, and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84068 provides live Linux evidence with no `handoff.log` and an unchanged version. I did not run a live systemd update in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Manual review and contributor action are needed because the external PR has blocking runtime findings and lacks live Linux systemd proof that automation cannot supply for the contributor.

target.title: fix(gateway): escape systemd cgroup for update handoff helper
target.body:
## Summary

On systemd-supervised installs, the managed-service update handoff spawns a detached Node.js child process (the "handoff helper") that runs `openclaw update --yes` after the gateway exits. However, with `KillMode=control-group` in the systemd unit, **all processes in the service cgroup are killed** during restart ã including the detached helper. The update never completes.

A secondary issue: the gateway exits with code 0 (triggering `Restart=always`), so systemd immediately restarts the gateway on old code before the update has a chance to run.

## Changes

### 1. systemd unit template (`src/daemon/systemd-unit.ts`)
- `KillMode=control-group` ã `KillMode=mixed` (SIGTERM only to main process; SIGKILL to cgroup after TimeoutStopSec)
- `RestartPreventExitStatus=78` ã `RestartPreventExitStatus=78 80` (exit 80 = update in progress)
- `StartLimitBurst=5` ã `StartLimitBurst=8` (accommodate update restart cycles)
- `StartLimitIntervalSec=60` ã `StartLimitIntervalSec=120`
- Added `ExecStartPre=-/bin/sh -c 'test ! -f %t/openclaw-update-in-progress || sleep 5'`

### 2. Update handoff spawn (`src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.ts`)
- Detect systemd supervisor before spawning handoff helper
- Under systemd: use `systemd-run --user --scope --unit=openclaw-update-handoff` to spawn in an independent transient scope unit that survives service restart
- Non-systemd: unchanged (detached spawn)
- Handoff script writes `/tmp/openclaw-update-in-progress` marker, cleans up in finally block

### 3. Respawn logic (`src/infra/process-respawn.ts`, `src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts`)
- For systemd update handoff: return exit code 80 instead of 0
- Exit 80 is covered by `RestartPreventExitStatus`, preventing premature restart

## Tests

- `src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts`: Verifies KillMode=mixed, StartLimitBurst=8, RestartPreventExitStatus includes 80
- `src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts`: 14 tests including systemd-run scope spawn, marker file lifecycle, non-systemd fallback
- `src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts`: Verifies exit code 80 for systemd update path

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Update handoff helper is killed by systemd `KillMode=control-group` during service restart, making the Update button a no-op on systemd installs.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `acd536eef4`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `systemd-unit.test.ts` asserts `KillMode=control-group` and `StartLimitBurst=5`. The update-handoff tests do not cover systemd-run scope spawning.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts
```
- **Evidence after fix**:
```
 ã unit-fast ../../src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts (4 tests) 2ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)

 ã gateway-client ../../src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests) 625ms
 ã gateway-methods ../../src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests) 650ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  14 passed (14)

 ã infra ../../src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts (20 tests) 19ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  20 passed (20)
```
- **Observed result after fix**: 38 tests pass across 4 test files covering the systemd unit template changes, the scope-based handoff spawn under systemd detection, and the exit code 80 respawn path.
- **What was not tested**: No live systemd service tested. Tests use mocked `detectRespawnSupervisor()` and `child_process.spawn()`. The `systemd-run --user --scope` invocation is verified via mock assertions on spawn arguments. Actual cgroup escape behavior requires a real systemd host.

Fixes #84068
target.author: jerry-xin

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
